project.name: ai-toolkit
project.description: VS Code extension providing AI-oriented tools, starting with a code annotation workflow that persists notes attached to code ranges for agent consumption and VS Code comments projection.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: npm registry configuration restricting package publishing.

nodes.name: .vscode-test.mjs
nodes.summary: VS Code extension test runner configuration.

nodes.name: .vscodeignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files to exclude from the packaged VS Code extension.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Release history tracking version changes and improvements.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Canonical domain glossary defining ubiquitous language for the AI Toolkit annotation workflow.

nodes.name: annotation-v1-implementation-plan.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation plan for the annotation v1 feature describing architecture decisions, phases, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration defining linting rules for the TypeScript codebase.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest defining VS Code extension metadata, dependencies, activation events, and contribution points.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and getting-started guide for the AI Toolkit VS Code extension.

nodes.name: ai-toolkit.annotations.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema definition for the annotation store file format, validating structure and field types.

nodes.name: run-vscode-test.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI test runner script that retries VS Code extension tests with configurable retry logic and subprocess management.

nodes.name: extension.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code extension entry point exposing activate and deactivate lifecycle hooks.

nodes.name: annotationContextKeys.ts
nodes.summary: Registers VS Code context keys that drive conditional UI visibility and command availability for the annotation feature.

nodes.name: registerAnnotationFeature.ts
nodes.summary: Top-level feature registration composing all annotation subsystems — storage, commands, projections, and watchers — into the VS Code extension context.

nodes.name: anchorMatching.ts
nodes.summary: Domain logic for creating and re-anchoring annotation anchors to code ranges using fingerprint-based fuzzy matching.

nodes.name: annotationModels.ts
nodes.summary: Core domain model types and factory functions for annotation stores, sessions, entries, and anchors.

nodes.name: annotationSchema.ts
nodes.summary: Schema constants and path helpers for locating annotation store and backup files within workspace folders.

nodes.name: annotationValidation.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive runtime validation for annotation store JSON including schema version checks, session structure, entry integrity, and range validation.

nodes.name: draftShapes.ts
nodes.summary: Draft output shape types and constants representing the structured annotation data for export formats (Markdown, JSON, YAML).

nodes.name: log.ts
nodes.summary: Structured logging utilities for annotation subsystem events including store reloads, conflicts, and invalid store warnings.

nodes.name: workspaceFolders.ts
nodes.summary: Helpers to resolve the relevant VS Code workspace folder from the active editor or palette command context.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode for the extension source.

nodes.name: vsc-extension-quickstart.md
nodes.summary: VS Code extension quickstart guide generated by the extension scaffolding tool.

nodes.name: annotationWorkspaceService.ts
nodes.summary: Central application service managing annotation workspace state — sessions, entries, anchors, commits, and draft generation — with reactive state change events.

nodes.name: draftOutputService.ts
nodes.summary: Generates formatted draft output (Markdown, JSON, YAML) from annotation projection data grouped by file.

nodes.name: projectionModel.ts
nodes.summary: Derives an immutable read-only workspace projection from raw annotation store state for consumption by presentation layer components.

nodes.name: sessionSelectionService.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates session creation and selection workflows, ensuring an active session exists before annotation operations proceed.

nodes.name: annotationBackupService.ts
nodes.summary: Creates timestamped annotation store backups and prunes old backups to the configured retention limit.

nodes.name: annotationMigrations.ts
nodes.summary: Detects and migrates annotation store data from older schema versions to the current format.

nodes.name: annotationStorageController.ts
nodes.summary: Manages reading and writing the annotation store JSON file with queued writes, backup integration, and schema migration support.

nodes.name: annotationStoreWatcher.ts
nodes.summary: Watches the annotation store file for external changes and triggers workspace service refresh.

nodes.name: annotationCodeLensProvider.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code CodeLens provider that shows annotation actions inline with annotated code ranges.

nodes.name: annotationCommands.ts
nodes.summary: Registers and implements all VS Code annotation commands — add/edit, select session, dismiss, reanchor, purge, and draft generation.

nodes.name: annotationCommentProjectionService.ts
nodes.summary: Projects annotation entries as VS Code comment threads providing in-editor annotation visibility.

nodes.name: annotationInput.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code input box service for capturing annotation body text with pre-fill support for edit workflows.

nodes.name: annotationTargeting.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves editor selections to workspace-relative file paths, anchors, and annotation lookup for command targeting.

nodes.name: sessionQuickPick.ts
nodes.summary: Creates VS Code QuickPick items for review session selection and implements the session picker presenter.

nodes.name: extension.test.ts
nodes.summary: Extension-level integration test verifying basic extension activation.

nodes.name: anchorMatching.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for anchor matching and re-anchoring algorithms.

nodes.name: annotationCommands.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for annotation command handlers with fake workspace service and editor API stubs.

nodes.name: annotationProjection.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for annotation projection to VS Code comment threads.

nodes.name: annotationStorageController.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for annotation storage controller including reads, writes, migration, and backup behavior.

nodes.name: annotationValidation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for annotation store JSON validation covering valid and invalid shapes.

nodes.name: annotationWorkspaceService.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the annotation workspace service covering session lifecycle, annotation CRUD, and conflict resolution.

nodes.name: draftOutputService.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for draft output generation verifying Markdown, JSON, and YAML formatting.

layers.name: Documentation & Configuration
layers.description: Project documentation, configuration files, and build tooling. Includes README, CHANGELOG, planning docs, package manifest, TypeScript config, ESLint config, JSON schema, and extension packaging config.

layers.name: Extension Entry Point
layers.description: VS Code extension lifecycle hooks and top-level feature registration. The activate/deactivate entry point and the bootstrap that wires all annotation subsystems into the extension context.

layers.name: Domain
layers.description: Core domain model, validation logic, schema constants, and anchor-matching algorithms. Pure business logic with no VS Code dependencies — defines the annotation lifecycle, data shapes, and structural invariants.

layers.name: Application
layers.description: Application services orchestrating domain operations: workspace state management, session selection, draft output generation, and projection derivation. Mediates between domain and infrastructure/presentation layers.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Persistence and file-system adapters: storage controller for reading/writing the annotation JSON store, file watcher for external changes, backup service, and schema migrations.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.description: VS Code UI layer: commands, CodeLens provider, comment thread projections, session quick-pick, annotation input, and editor targeting. All components that interact directly with the VS Code API.

layers.name: Utilities
layers.description: Cross-cutting utility helpers: structured logging and workspace folder resolution.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Automated test suite: integration test for extension activation and unit tests covering domain validation, storage, commands, projections, workspace service, and draft output.

layers.name: Tooling & Scripts
layers.description: Build, test, and packaging scripts: the VS Code test runner script and VS Code test configuration.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and domain glossary to understand the purpose of AI Toolkit and its annotation workflow. The CONTEXT.md defines the ubiquitous language used throughout the codebase — key terms like Annotation, Review Session, Anchor, and Comment Projection.

tour.title: Extension Entry Point
tour.description: The extension activates through src/extension.ts which delegates immediately to registerAnnotationFeature. This bootstrap function wires together all subsystems — storage, commands, watchers, and projections — into VS Code's extension context using disposables.

tour.title: Domain Model
tour.description: The domain layer defines the core data shapes and business rules. annotationModels.ts holds the fundamental types (stores, sessions, entries, anchors). annotationValidation.ts enforces store integrity at runtime. annotationSchema.ts provides file-path constants. draftShapes.ts defines the export format shapes.

tour.title: Anchor Matching Algorithm
tour.description: anchorMatching.ts implements the fingerprint-based fuzzy re-anchoring algorithm. When a file changes, this code finds the best match for each annotation's anchor using a multi-strategy approach: exact range match, fingerprint similarity scoring, and selected-text candidate search.

tour.title: Application Services
tour.description: The application layer orchestrates domain operations. AnnotationWorkspaceService is the central stateful service managing sessions, annotations, and commits. SessionSelectionService ensures an active session before operations proceed. projectionModel.ts derives the read-only view consumed by UI components. draftOutputService.ts formats annotations for export.

tour.title: Persistence Infrastructure
tour.description: The infrastructure layer handles all file I/O. AnnotationStorageController manages queued reads and writes of the JSON store file with migration support. AnnotationStoreWatcher detects external file changes and triggers refresh. AnnotationBackupService creates timestamped backups and prunes old ones.

tour.title: VS Code UI Commands
tour.description: annotationCommands.ts registers all user-facing commands: add/edit annotation, select session, dismiss, reanchor, purge dismissed, and generate draft output. Each command follows a consistent pattern: resolve workspace folder, ensure ready state, invoke the service, and report results.

tour.title: VS Code UI Projections
tour.description: Two projection components keep the editor UI in sync with the annotation state. AnnotationCommentProjectionService renders annotations as VS Code comment threads. AnnotationCodeLensProvider shows action lenses inline on annotated code ranges.

tour.title: Test Suite
tour.description: The test suite covers domain validation, storage, commands, projections, workspace service, and draft output with unit tests that use fake implementations for VS Code APIs and storage. The extension integration test verifies basic activation.
